message:
refactor(frankenlibc-abi): route errno reads/writes through ABI helpers and complete startup checkpoint ordering test

Three small but coordinated ABI cleanups.

1) `unistd_abi.rs` — All sem_*, getpriority, aio_submit, and fts_read
   errno interactions now go through the ABI-level helpers
   `set_abi_errno(errno)` for writes and a new inline
   `current_abi_errno()` for reads, instead of poking
   `libc::__errno_location()` directly.

   Mixing raw `*libc::__errno_location()` writes with `set_abi_errno()`
   writes elsewhere in the crate is a landmine: the two paths alias the
   same thread-local slot in most builds, but the crate is compiled
   against configurations (musl, sanitizers, TLS-wrapper shims) where
   they can drift, and at least one subtle sem_* EOVERFLOW regression
   was traced to exactly that drift. Funneling everything through the
   ABI helpers makes the rule uniform: ABI errno is set by ABI helpers
   and read by ABI helpers. No exceptions inside this crate.

   `current_abi_errno()` is a `#[inline]` that does a volatile read off
   `crate::errno_abi::__errno_location()`, mirroring the volatile write
   pattern inside `set_abi_errno()`. The volatility matters because the
   compiler must not CSE an errno read across a potentially errno-
   setting call.

   Call sites converted: sem_init, sem_destroy, sem_post, sem_wait
   (including the EINTR surface after sem_futex_wait), sem_trywait,
   sem_timedwait (both EINVAL and the ETIMEDOUT / EINTR surfaces),
   sem_getvalue, getpriority, aio_submit, and fts_read. No semantic
   change; all of these continue to write the same errno values they
   wrote before.

2) `resolv_abi.rs` — Single-line comment noting that gai_cancel,
   gai_error, and gai_suspend live in unistd_abi.rs, not in resolv.
   Cheap documentation; avoids another fifteen minutes of grep the
   next time someone looks for them here.

3) `startup_helpers.rs` — The startup checkpoint ordering test was
   hand-listing the expected sequence of `StartupCheckpoint` variants
   but stopped at `CaptureInvariants`, `CallMain`, `Complete`. The
   actual runtime sequence gained several steps since that test was
   written: `ResolveEnvp`, `BindProcessGlobals`, `BootstrapHostSymbols`,
   `InitHostStdio`, `BootstrapHostLibio`, `PrewarmThreadSymbols`,
   `PrewarmAllocatorSymbols`, `SignalRuntimeReady`. Insert them between
   `CaptureInvariants` and `CallMain` in the expected vec so the test
   actually asserts the full checkpoint order the runtime executes,
   not a truncated prefix.

files.patch:
@@ -747,6 +747,14 @@ mod tests {
             StartupCheckpoint::ScanAuxvVector,
             StartupCheckpoint::ClassifySecureMode,
             StartupCheckpoint::CaptureInvariants,
+            StartupCheckpoint::ResolveEnvp,
+            StartupCheckpoint::BindProcessGlobals,
+            StartupCheckpoint::BootstrapHostSymbols,
+            StartupCheckpoint::InitHostStdio,
+            StartupCheckpoint::BootstrapHostLibio,
+            StartupCheckpoint::PrewarmThreadSymbols,
+            StartupCheckpoint::PrewarmAllocatorSymbols,
+            StartupCheckpoint::SignalRuntimeReady,
             StartupCheckpoint::CallMain,
             StartupCheckpoint::Complete,
         ];
